topic: Schools should serve only vegetarian meals at least once a week

systemArguments.text: Vegetarian meals reduce the environmental impact of school food programs by lowering carbon emissions
Plant-based meals can be healthier for students, reducing obesity and promoting better eating habits
Vegetarian days expose students to diverse cuisines and expand their cultural awareness
Schools can save money on vegetarian meals since plant proteins are typically less expensive than meat
Weekly vegetarian meals teach students about sustainable food choices and environmental responsibility
Vegetarian options accommodate students with religious dietary restrictions who cannot eat certain meats
Plant-based meals reduce the risk of foodborne illnesses associated with meat preparation and storage
Vegetarian days can reduce food waste since plant-based ingredients have longer shelf lives

goldenArguments.text: Society has a responsibility to protect the environment for future generations
Schools should promote compassion and respect for all living beings
Educational institutions have a duty to teach sustainable and ethical practices
Inclusive meal programs ensure all students can participate regardless of religious beliefs
Vegetarian meals improve student health outcomes and reduce childhood obesity
Plant-based school meals reduce environmental impact and carbon footprint
Weekly vegetarian days lower food costs and improve budget efficiency
Diverse meal options expand students' cultural knowledge and food literacy

humanRationale: System arguments provide solid practical benefits covering environmental impact, health, cost savings, cultural exposure, and food safety. However, they focus primarily on utilitarian outcomes rather than underlying values. The golden arguments better separate moral imperatives (environmental responsibility, compassion, educational duty, inclusivity) from policy benefits (health, environment, costs, cultural learning). System captures most policy dimensions well but misses the deeper ethical foundations that drive support for this position.
